_comment: DRAFT seccomp profile for a NETWORK-LESS render worker (roadmap, issue #2).
This is deny-by-default (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO): only the listed syscalls are allowed,
and networking syscalls (socket/connect/bind/sendto/...) are deliberately omitted,
so scene code cannot open a network connection even via a future syscall path.
It is intended for a dedicated render service that ONLY runs renderer.render() —
NOT for the web container, which needs sockets to serve HTTP and call the Anthropic
API. Until rendering is split into its own service (issue #2), the web container
should keep Docker's DEFAULT seccomp profile (applied automatically).
Curated for CPython 3.12 + Pillow on x86_64. TEST under your exact kernel/arch
(strace the worker, add any EPERM/ENOSYS syscalls it actually needs) before
relying on it. Apply with: security_opt: [seccomp:./seccomp/render-worker.json].
